rationale: Within a ~9-second window, a parent cmd.exe (PID 1208, EV-001/EV-002) spawned two separate cmd→powershell chains executing local user enumeration commands: 'Get-LocalUser' (A0, EV-001) and 'Get-WmiObject Win32_UserAccount' (EV-002, EV-003). The rapid, sequential, programmatic-style invocation of two distinct user-enumeration techniques via cmd /c wrappers is consistent with automated post-exploitation discovery activity (T1087.001). However, all processes run at Medium integrity under corp\user-01 with legitimate system binaries, no obfuscation, no encoded payloads, and no evidence of lateral movement, persistence, or data exfiltration. The grandparent cmd.exe (PID 1208) context is unknown — it could be a script, pentest tool, or interactive session — and absence of that context caps confidence. The pattern warrants investigation to determine what spawned PID 1208 and whether this is authorized activity.
